title: Authors and Citation
Authors and Citation
text: Lightweight (2026). lightweightchartR: Pipe-First TradingView Lightweight Charts Quantmod Workflows. R package version 0.1.0, https://arkraieski.github.io/lightweightchartR/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {lightweightchartR: Pipe-First TradingView Lightweight Charts for Quantmod Workflows},   author = {Alex Lightweight},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://arkraieski.github.io/lightweightchartR/}, }

title: lightweightchartR
text: lightweightchartR brings TradingView’s lightweight-charts library R package designed quantmod users. goal simple: make interactive market charts feel natural R keep workflow pipe-friendly support common trading-chart patterns like volume overlays, moving averages, indicator panes without lot setup

title: lightweightchartR
text: already work quantmod, xts, pipe-based workflows, package gives modern interactive charting layer keeping data pipeline familiar. Instead building chart specs hand, can :
code: library(quantmod) library(lightweightchartR)  getSymbols("SPY", from = "2024-01-01")  SPY |>   lwc_chart(theme = "dark", name = "SPY") |>   add_volume() |>   add_sma(50, color = "blue") |>   add_sma(200, color = "goldenrod") |>   add_rsi()

title: lightweightchartR
text: TradingView lightweight-charts powered htmlwidget Pipe-first API Works naturally xts, zoo, quantmod Candles, bars, lines, areas, histograms Volume overlays main price pane Technical indicators like SMA, EMA, BBands, RSI, MACD Multi-pane charts Hover tooltips technical overlays price pane Shiny support small compatibility bridge chartSeries()-style workflows

title: lightweightchartR
code: library(quantmod) library(lightweightchartR)  getSymbols("AAPL", from = "2023-01-01")  AAPL |>   lwc_chart(theme = "dark", name = "AAPL") |>   add_volume() |>   add_sma(20, color = "#2563eb") |>   add_sma(50, color = "#f59e0b")

title: lightweightchartR
text: users migrating quantmod::chartSeries(), also compact wrapper:
code: chart_series_lwc(   AAPL,   theme = "dark",   TA = "addVo();addSMA(n = 20);addRSI()" )

title: lightweightchartR
code: pak::pak("arkraieski/lightweightchartR")

title: lightweightchartR
text: package currently focused : quantmod workflows clean defaults market-chart use cases small, practical API rather exposing every charting knob front advanced customization can added time clearly improves real workflows.

title: Add a generic indicator or comparison series — add_ta
text: chart lightweightchartR chart object. values xts, zoo, data frame object time column. type One "line", "area", "histogram". pane Target pane. Defaults "price". name Optional display name. color Optional series color. pane_height Relative pane height new panes. overlay Whether overlay existing price scale.

what: Compatibility wrapper for quantmod users — chart_series_lwc
title: Compatibility wrapper for quantmod users — chart_series_lwc
text: chart_series_lwc() offers quantmod-flavored bridge pipe-first chart API. recognizes small subset common chartSeries() addTA() workflows.

title: Compatibility wrapper for quantmod users — chart_series_lwc
code: chart_series_lwc(   x,   type = c("auto", "candles", "bars", "line", "area"),   subset = NULL,   show.grid = TRUE,   name = NULL,   log.scale = FALSE,   TA = NULL,   theme = c("light", "dark"),   up.col = "#26a69a",   dn.col = "#ef5350",   ... )

title: Compatibility wrapper for quantmod users — chart_series_lwc
text: x Market data. Usually xts object OHLC OHLCV columns. type Base series type. "auto" chooses candlesticks OHLC data available line chart otherwise. subset Optional quantmod-style subset string, passed xts. show.grid Included API familiarity. Currently ignored. name Optional display name chart. log.scale Included API familiarity. Currently ignored. TA Optional semicolon-delimited TA expressions like "addVo();addSMA(n = 20)". theme "light" "dark". .col, dn.col Rising falling colors price series. ... Reserved future compatibility arguments.

title: Compatibility wrapper for quantmod users — chart_series_lwc

code: lightweightchartROutput(outputId, width = "100%", height = "480px")  renderLightweightchartR(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for lightweightchartR — lightweightchartR-shiny
text: outputId Output variable read . width, height CSS dimensions widget container. expr expression generates chart. env environment evaluate expr. quoted expr already quoted?

title: Create a lightweight chart — lwc_chart
text: lwc_chart() creates TradingView lightweight chart widget xts, zoo, data frame inputs. result pipeable chart object can extended add_*() helpers.

title: Create a lightweight chart — lwc_chart
code: lwc_chart(   data,   type = c("auto", "candles", "bars", "line", "area"),   subset = NULL,   name = NULL,   theme = c("light", "dark"),   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   elementId = NULL,   up_col = "#26a69a",   down_col = "#ef5350",   ta_tooltip = TRUE,   ta_tooltip_threshold = 14 )

title: Create a lightweight chart — lwc_chart
text: data Market data. Usually xts object OHLC OHLCV columns. type Base series type. "auto" chooses candlesticks OHLC data available line chart otherwise. subset Optional quantmod-style subset string, passed xts. name Optional display name chart. theme "light" "dark". width, height Widget dimensions passed htmlwidgets. elementId Optional element id. up_col, down_col Rising/falling color defaults price series. ta_tooltip Whether show hover tooltips price-pane technical analysis overlays. ta_tooltip_threshold Pixel distance used match pointer nearest overlay line TA tooltip.

title: Create a lightweight chart — lwc_chart
text: htmlwidget object also supports pipe-based layer helpers.

title: Reconfigure an existing chart — rechart_lwc
text: rechart_lwc() updates global display options existing chart.

title: Reconfigure an existing chart — rechart_lwc
code: rechart_lwc(chart, theme = NULL, ...)

title: Reconfigure an existing chart — rechart_lwc
text: chart lightweightchartR chart object. theme Optional replacement theme. ... Additional lightweight-charts chart options merged existing top-level options.

title: Reconfigure an existing chart — rechart_lwc
